market_direction.summary: Domestic white-collar demand remains uneven, with selective hiring resilience in continuity-linked roles.
evidence: Hiring momentum appears concentrated by function rather than broad-based.
Transition outcomes improve when role continuity and timing discipline are preserved.

competition_pressure.summary: Competition remains moderate for senior profiles with adjacent-role portability and clear scope framing.
evidence: Candidates with directly portable responsibilities tend to convert faster.
Ambiguous role framing increases competitive pressure during transitions.

economic_pressure.summary: Macro uncertainty creates selective budget discipline rather than broad hiring shutdown.
evidence: Headcount pacing remains cautious under volatile demand visibility.
Compensation normalization pressure persists outside mission-critical tracks.

transition_friction.summary: Transition friction is manageable under bounded sequencing, but increases with prolonged ambiguity.
evidence: Long search windows raise runway strain when target scope is overly broad.

source_notes: Perplexity Sonar synthesis across labor and macro signals.
Interpretation constrained to AMC external-layer contract v1.
